plan_under_test: Add a strict companion-input mode to asset-reference validation so release-style checks can hard-fail when persisted manifest/export sidecars are missing.
tests_added_or_changed: Added test_asset_reference_validation_strict_companion_inputs_fail_when_sidecars_are_missing. The fixture writes only question_bank.json plus canonical images, then calls validate_asset_references(strict_companion_inputs=True) and expects ok=false, no warnings, and five missing_validation_input errors.
expected_initial_failure: Before implementation, validate_asset_references does not accept strict_companion_inputs, so the new test should fail with an unexpected keyword argument.
what_passing_proves: Local/default validation can still pass with warnings when generated companion outputs are absent.
Strict validation can be used as a hard release gate for persisted companion outputs.
The strictness choice is explicit rather than inferred from output state.
what_passing_does_not_prove: It does not generate the missing sidecars.
It does not prove the sidecars are semantically correct when present.
It does not validate PNG visual content.
risk_of_bad_tests: Low. The test exercises the public validator directly and checks exact error labels for the missing companion inputs.
